seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in n8n AI Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in n8n AI Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In n8n AI Agents, hallucinations manifest as AI nodes generating fabricated facts or incorrect tool call parameters that propagate unchecked through workflow chains, leading to erroneous actions. Common triggers include unvalidated outputs from AI Agent nodes passed directly to HTTP Request or Execute Command nodes, and recursive loops from workflow triggers amplifying bad data. This is exacerbated by n8n's node-based architecture where outputs flow automatically without built-in semantic validation.

howItManifests.scenario: AI Agent node outputs unverified facts to downstream HTTP Request node
howItManifests.symptom: Agent claims 'API endpoint is https://api.fakebank.com/transfer' which doesn't exist
howItManifests.impact: Failed API calls and exposed error logs with hallucinated credentials

howItManifests.scenario: Recursive workflow trigger from AI node output
howItManifests.symptom: Agent generates circular tool calls like 'call workflow again with same params'
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops consuming credits and generating hallucinated response chains

howItManifests.scenario: Memory window node injects stale hallucinated context
howItManifests.symptom: Conversation memory contains 'User balance: $1M' from prior hallucination
howItManifests.impact: Agent makes decisions based on fabricated financial data

howItManifests.scenario: Tool call parameters contain hallucinated auth tokens
howItManifests.symptom: AI generates 'Bearer sk-12345fakekey' in tool parameters
howItManifests.impact: Credential exposure in workflow logs and failed auth attempts

detection.name: Semantic Fact-Check Node
detection.description: n8n Code node that validates AI output against known facts using simple embedding distance

detection.name: Tool Parameter Schema Validator
detection.description: Validates AI-generated tool call parameters against expected JSON schema

detection.name: Recursion Loop Detector
detection.description: Detects recursive patterns in AI agent outputs that could trigger workflow loops

fixes.name: Fallback Fact Injection Node
fixes.description: Code node that corrects hallucinated outputs with verified facts before downstream actions

fixes.name: Safe Tool Call Wrapper
fixes.description: Sanitizes AI-generated tool parameters before execution

fixes.name: Loop Prevention Gate
fixes.description: Blocks recursive calls and injects loop-breaking instructions

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly into n8n workflows as a webhook node placed immediately after AI Agent nodes, automatically detecting hallucinations using semantic validation, schema enforcement, and behavioral pattern analysis. It provides real-time scoring of outputs (0-1 hallucination risk) and automatic remediation by injecting corrected facts or blocking dangerous tool calls before they reach action nodes. For n8n's common failure modes like recursive triggers and memory injection, Vex maintains workflow-specific fact bases and call graphs to prevent propagation of bad data across complex agentic chains.

The Vex webhook receives n8n's JSON payload, runs it through specialized hallucination detectors tuned for AI Agent node outputs, and returns either 'PASS' with cleaned data or 'BLOCK' with remediation instructions. This prevents credential exposure, failed API calls, and infinite loops without requiring manual code node configuration. Vex also provides a dashboard showing hallucination trends across all n8n workflows, helping teams identify problematic AI models or prompt patterns.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
